title: toona ciliata var. hexandra
intro: Toona ciliata var. hexandra, also known as the forest mahogany or red mahogany, is a deciduous tree native to Australia and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Meliaceae. The medicinal properties of Toona ciliata var. hexandra lie in its bark, leaves, and seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating conditions like fever, cough, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Toona ciliata var. hexandra include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
